# Execution Plan: Mirage + Neotoma Skills & Activation

**Status:** Draft for execution  
**Source analysis:** `docs/private/strategy/mirage_skills_activation_strategy.md`  
**Related:** `docs/private/competitive/mirage_competitive_analysis.md`, `docs/private/partnerships/mirage_partnership_analysis.md`  
**Last updated:** 2026-05-11

---

## Goals

1. Reduce activation time to first value by centralizing external data access (optional Mirage path before per-service MCPs).
2. Keep Mirage **optional** — all `remember-*` skills work without it (MCP → local files → manual).
3. Preserve State Layer boundaries: Mirage = access/orchestration in the agent harness; Neotoma = `store` / provenance / deterministic state only.
4. Ship documentation and one proof-of-concept before any hard dependency on Mirage APIs.

---

## Non-goals

- Embedding Mirage inside the Neotoma server process.
- Replacing Neotoma MCP with Mirage as the only integration surface.
- Automatic ingestion without preview + explicit user confirmation (unchanged from `install.md`).

---

## Phase 0 — Preconditions & risk

| Task | Owner | Done when |
|------|-------|-----------|
| P0.1 Track Mirage releases (semver, breaking changes, mount path conventions) | Maintainer | Changelog reviewed before each phase-1+ merge |
| P0.2 Confirm dual-path contract in `docs/skills/skill_strategy.md` (Mirage optional, never required for npm install) | Maintainer | Doc PR merged |

**Exit criteria:** No Neotoma release blocks on Mirage availability.

---

## Phase 1 — Documentation & contracts (near-term)

| ID | Task | Acceptance criteria |
|----|------|---------------------|
| P1.1 | Publish **“Mirage + Neotoma skills”** integration guide (site or `docs/developer/`): prerequisites, mount examples, path priority (Mirage → MCP → files), preview/confirm unchanged | Linked from `install.md` “Available skills” or skills index; no PII in examples |
| P1.2 | Update **`docs/skills/skill_strategy.md`** (or canonical skills doc): document runtime path selection for `remember-email` / `remember-calendar` as reference pattern | Other `remember-*` skills list “same pattern” or inherit from template |
| P1.3 | Add **“Connected discovery”** subsection to `install.md` activation step 2 (optional branch: *if* Mirage mounts present, include in domain discovery; still shallow, user-scoped) | Guardrails unchanged: no silent ingest; preview contract still mandatory |

**Exit criteria:** A new user (or agent) can follow the guide without reading the private strategy doc.

---

## Phase 2 — Proof of concept (single skill)

| ID | Task | Acceptance criteria |
|----|------|---------------------|
| P2.1 | Implement **Mirage-aware path** in `remember-email` skill only: detect Mirage workspace + `/gmail/` (or documented mount) → read sample → same extraction + `store` contract as Gmail MCP path | Skill SKILL.md updated with detection steps, fallbacks, and provenance string pattern |
| P2.2 | **Manual test script** or checklist: Mirage off (MCP path), Mirage on (filesystem path), neither (export file path) | Recorded in `docs/developer/` or skill appendix; no automated dependency on Mirage in CI unless containerized |
| P2.3 | **Optional:** example Mirage workspace YAML (redacted) in repo `examples/` or docs | Runs on maintainer machine; not required in default CI |

**Exit criteria:** At least one successful end-to-end ingest from Mirage mount with same preview → confirm → timeline aha as file-only path.

---

## Phase 3 — Extend skills (medium-term)

| ID | Task | Acceptance criteria |
|----|------|---------------------|
| P3.1 | Apply dual-path pattern to `remember-calendar`, `remember-conversations` (Slack via mount where applicable), `remember-finances`, `remember-contacts`, `remember-codebase` | Each skill documents mount assumptions + fallback order |
| P3.2 | Evaluate **new** packaged skills (`remember-slack`, `remember-drive`, etc.) only after P3.1 stable and Mirage API stable | Each new skill has same preview/confirm + provenance rules |
| P3.3 | **`neotoma setup` / doctor**: optional hint when Mirage not detected (“faster email/calendar import with Mirage — see link”) | No new mandatory prompts; hint is one line in doctor JSON or post-setup summary |

**Exit criteria:** All shipped `remember-*` skills list three-tier path where external services apply.

---

## Phase 4 — Activation product (medium-term)

| ID | Task | Acceptance criteria |
|----|------|---------------------|
| P4.1 | Add onboarding mode **“connected”** (opt-in): discovery includes Mirage mount roots when user confirms Mirage in use | Stored as `user_preference`; default remains quick win / local |
| P4.2 | Update **`docs/developer/agent_onboarding_confirmation.md`** Stage 2 for connected mode (domain clustering includes remote paths as pseudo-domains) | Examples show “Gmail thread domain” without exposing message bodies in logs |
| P4.3 | Reconcile with **`neotoma discover`** CLI if extended for remote — or document “agent-only” discovery for mounts | No full-disk scan; user-scoped paths only |

**Exit criteria:** Connected mode passes same preview contract and Installation Aha format as local-only.

---

## Phase 5 — Deeper integration (long-term / conditional)

**Gate:** Mirage stable ≥ agreed threshold (e.g. minor version, documented mount API, 8+ weeks without breaking mount paths).

| ID | Task | Acceptance criteria |
|----|------|---------------------|
| P5.1 | Explore **Neotoma as Mirage resource** (`/memory`) — separate RFC / ADR; no default product commitment | ADR in `docs/architecture/` or private strategy |
| P5.2 | Optional **`neotoma setup --with-mirage`** or documented compose recipe | Idempotent; documented rollback |
| P5.3 | Partner touchpoint with Strukto (co-example repo or blog) | Public artifact if partnership proceeds |

**Exit criteria:** Only after gate; items may remain “exploratory” indefinitely.

---

## Measurement

| Metric | Baseline | Target after P2 |
|--------|----------|-------------------|
| Time to first structured ingest from Gmail (median, internal dogfood) | 10–14 min (MCP setup) | ≤7 min with Mirage pre-configured |
| % activations completing without external MCP | Higher | Track via optional analytics or manual study |

---

## Dependencies

- User (or operator) configures Mirage credentials and mounts; Neotoma does not ship Mirage.
- Agent harness must allow Mirage `execute` or equivalent (or pre-sync export to temp files) without violating `install.md` permission strategy — document any extra allowlist lines.

---

## Rollback

- Remove Mirage sections from skills; documentation remains accurate for MCP-only users.
- No database migration required (Neotoma schema unchanged).

---

## Checklist for “plan saved in Neotoma”

When persisting this plan via `store`:

- [ ] Store a **`plan`** entity with `plan_key` (stable slug), `schema_version`, `title`, and **`body` = full markdown** of this document (canonical durable copy in structured state).
- [ ] Optionally attach this file (`file_path` or `file_content`) as a **source** for byte-identical provenance alongside the `plan` row.
- [ ] Link **`REFERS_TO`** from the prompting `conversation_message` to the `plan` entity.
- [ ] Optionally link **`REFERS_TO`** to existing `Mirage` / `Strukto AI` entities and to any stored analysis artifact for `mirage_skills_activation_strategy.md`.
